description: chrony is a daemon which implements the Network Time Protocol (NTP). It is designed to synchronize system clocks across a variety of systems and use a source that is highly accurate. More information on chrony can be found at https://chrony-project.org/. Chrony can be configured to be a client and/or a server. To ensure that chronyd is running under chrony user account, remove any -u ... option from OPTIONS other than -u chrony, as chrony is run under its own user by default. This recommendation only applies if chrony is in use on the system.
rationale:
If chrony is in use on the system proper configuration is vital to ensuring time synchronization
is working properly.
ocil_clause: chronyd is not running under chrony user account
title: Ensure that chronyd is running under chrony user account
